title: https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus FUGUE/Kernelに必要か、批判的吟味せよ
summary_text: Findings: - `Adopt`: the **request schema concepts** (`source`, `priority`, `deadline`, `dependsOn`, `dagId`) and **diff-ledger pattern** for provider/API drift. These align with FUGUE’s workflow-facing role. - `Adopt with constraints`: lightweight workflow telemetry (run outcomes, drift flags) only if auto-collected from existing run artifacts; avoid manual logging steps. - `Do not adopt as-is`: `JSONL queue + TTL file lock` from `queue.js` as orchestration authority. In FUGUE this creates a second control plane and weakens ownership boundaries. - `Control-plane hygiene risk`: importing bus CLI semantics (`enqueue/dispatch`) into FUGUE routing path causes split truth, ambiguous retries, and harder incident recovery. - `Operational smoothness risk`: if teams must maintain both FUGUE state and bus state, latency and cognitive load increase; failure diagnosis becomes two-system correlation work. Score (FUGUE selective adoption): **7.1/10** Verdict: **GO (selective only)**. Use schema + audit patterns, but keep FUGUE’s existing control plane as the single authority.
